items.description: Windows-side staging can be created from the WSL repo so Windows tooling does not build from a \\wsl.localhost UNC working directory.
steps: From WSL, create a temporary Windows-path staging copy or worktree for desktop build and run steps.
Run a Windows-side command against the staged desktop folder and verify it executes from a drive-backed path instead of a UNC working directory.
items.regression_notes: Verified with `.venv/bin/python -m parakeet.cli gui-stage --json` plus `cmd.exe /v:on /c "cd /d <staged-desktop-path> && echo !CD! && if exist package.json echo PACKAGE_OK"`, which resolved to a `C:\Users\...` staging path and confirmed the staged desktop manifest was present.

items.description: The repo exposes a deterministic Windows packaging command for the Electrobun desktop app from the WSL-controlled workflow.
steps: From WSL, run the documented Windows packaging command.
Verify an unsigned Windows x64 app artifact is produced at a documented path.
items.regression_notes: Verified with `.venv/bin/python -m parakeet.cli gui-package --json`, which staged `desktop/electrobun` to `%LOCALAPPDATA%`, ran `bun install` plus `bunx electrobun build --env=stable` on Windows, and produced the unsigned installer at `build/stable-win-x64/parakeet-desktop-Setup.exe` plus `artifacts/stable-win-x64-parakeet-desktop-Setup.zip`.

items.description: The packaged Windows app can launch unattended and expose startup diagnostics without user interaction.
steps: Launch the packaged Windows app with E2E automation enabled.
Verify the renderer and RPC layer initialize successfully and the process can be shut down cleanly with captured logs.
items.regression_notes: Verified with `.venv/bin/python -m parakeet.cli gui-package-smoke --json --timeout-seconds 120`, which staged and packaged the app, installed `parakeet-desktop-Setup.exe`, launched the installed Windows app unattended, captured startup logs plus diagnostics under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\ParakeetDictation\staging\...\smoke`, confirmed `bunReady=true`, `rendererReady=true`, and `rendererRpcReady=true`, then requested a clean window close that recorded `shutdownReason=window-close`.

items.description: The real parakeet bridge supports deterministic E2E mode without microphone access or model loading.
steps: Start `parakeet bridge` in WSL with E2E env vars enabled.
Call the existing bridge session endpoints and verify deterministic transcript, state, and history payloads.
items.regression_notes: Verified with `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_bridge.py -k 'e2e or build_bridge_controller_from_namespace_reads_e2e_env'`, which launches the real CLI bridge process under `PARAKEET_E2E_MODE=1` and checks deterministic `/health`, `/session/start`, `/session/stop`, `/session/toggle`, and `/session/clear-history` behavior. Also verified directly from WSL by starting `.venv/bin/python -u -m parakeet.cli bridge --host 127.0.0.1 --port 40124` with `PARAKEET_E2E_*` env vars and asserting deterministic transcript, recording state, and history payloads over HTTP without microphone/model access.

items.description: The desktop app exposes a minimal localhost-only automation surface when explicitly enabled for E2E.
steps: Launch the Windows app with automation env vars enabled.
Read app state and invoke only user-equivalent actions through the automation surface.
items.regression_notes: Verified with `.venv/bin/python -m parakeet.cli gui-package-automation --json --timeout-seconds 120`, which packaged and installed the Windows app, launched it with `PARAKEET_GUI_E2E=1` plus a Windows-selected localhost port, read `/state`, invoked `show-window`, `tray/open`, and `quit` over the localhost automation server, and captured diagnostics showing `automationReady=true`, tray/hotkey state, and `shutdownReason=automation:quit`. Regression check: `.venv/bin/python -m parakeet.cli gui-package-smoke --json --timeout-seconds 120` still passed with `shutdownReason=e2e-auto-exit`.

items.description: The desktop app recovers cleanly between offline and online bridge states without restart.
steps: Launch the GUI while the bridge is unavailable and verify a visible offline or error state plus bridge guidance.
Restore the bridge and verify the GUI reconnects without restarting.
items.regression_notes: Verified with `.venv/bin/python -m parakeet.cli gui-package-bridge-recovery --json --timeout-seconds 120`, which packaged and installed the Windows app, launched it with renderer automation enabled against an unused localhost bridge URL, confirmed a visible offline state (`Disconnected`, `Bridge offline`) plus the bridge command guidance in the renderer snapshot, then started the real deterministic WSL `parakeet bridge` on that port and observed the packaged GUI reconnect without restart to a ready `Start recording` state before quitting cleanly with `shutdownReason=automation:quit`.

items.description: The main window controls can start and stop a deterministic dictation session against the WSL bridge.
steps: Use the primary GUI control to start a session against the WSL bridge in deterministic E2E mode.
Use the GUI to stop the session and verify the deterministic transcript appears in visible history.

items.description: Tray menu actions can open the window, toggle recording, and quit the packaged app in unattended execution.
steps: Verify tray registration is reported by the app and trigger the tray open and toggle actions through automation.
Verify the tray quit action closes the app cleanly.

items.description: The configured global hotkey registers successfully and its callback path toggles recording end-to-end.
steps: Launch the packaged app with the configured hotkey and verify registration succeeds.
Trigger the hotkey callback path in unattended execution and verify recording toggles and produces the deterministic transcript.

items.description: The repo contains a single unattended local Windows plus WSL verification entrypoint for packaging and E2E.
steps: Run the repo's documented unattended verification command from WSL.
Verify it packages the app, exercises the Windows GUI plus WSL bridge flow, and exits 0 with useful logs.

items.description: Windows support documentation and repo knowledge files reflect the supported Windows GUI plus WSL bridge workflow.
steps: Follow the updated docs from a fresh WSL-first context to package and run the Windows GUI against the WSL bridge.
Verify the docs state support boundaries, WebView2 expectations, staging behavior, and the user-controlled lifecycle.
